78: def merge_ranges(ranges: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
79:     """Merge overlapping integer ranges. Returns sorted, non-overlapping ranges."""
80:     if not ranges:
81:         return []
82:     sorted_ranges = sorted(ranges)
83:     merged = [sorted_ranges[0]]
84:     for start, end in sorted_ranges[1:]:
85:         prev_start, prev_end = merged[-1]
86:         if start <= prev_end + 1:
87:             merged[-1] = (prev_start, max(prev_end, end))
88:         else:
89:             merged.append((start, end))

# src\osoji\scorecard.py
@source-hash: 8ea821580cab39d1
@impl-hash: 56478213dcceeeb9
@generated: 2026-07-01T20:28:09Z

## Purpose

`scorecard.py` aggregates outputs from all osoji audit phases into a single `Scorecard` dataclass holding headline metrics across five dimensions: documentation coverage, dead docs, accuracy, junk code, and enforcement. The single entry point `build_scorecard` is pure Python with no LLM calls.

---

## Dataclasses

### `CoverageEntry` (L14–17)
Per-source-file coverage entry:
- `source_path: str` — relative path string (forward-slash normalized)
- `topic_signature: dict | None` — loaded from `.osoji/signatures/` via `_load_signature`
- `covering_docs: list[dict]` — each dict: `{"path": str, "classification": str}`

### `JunkCodeEntry` (L21–26)
Per-source-file junk code summary:
- `source_path`, `total_lines`, `junk_lines`, `junk_fraction`, `items: list[dict]`
- `items` dicts contain keys: `category`, `line_start`, `line_end`, `source`, and optionally `confidence_source`, `name`, `kind`, `reason`, `remediation`, `confidence`

### `Scorecard` (L30–75)
Comprehensive audit result container with six metric groups:

| Group | Key fields |
|-------|-----------|
| Coverage (L32–38) | `coverage_entries`, `coverage_pct`, `covered_count`, `total_source_count`, `coverage_by_type`, `type_covered_counts`, `type_total_counts` |
| Dead docs (L41) | `dead_docs: list[str]` |
| Accuracy (L44–47) | `total_accuracy_errors`, `live_doc_count`, `accuracy_errors_per_doc`, `accuracy_by_category` |
| Junk code (L50–58) | `junk_total_lines`, `junk_total_source_lines`, `junk_fraction`, `junk_item_count`, `junk_file_count`, `junk_by_category`, `junk_by_category_lines`, `junk_entries`, `junk_sources` |
| Enforcement (L61–64) | `enforcement_total_obligations`, `enforcement_unactuated`, `enforcement_pct_unactuated`, `enforcement_by_schema` — all `None` if `--dead-plumbing` not run |
| Obligations / Concepts (L67–75) | `obligation_violations`, `obligation_implicit_contrac

# src\osoji/
@children-hash: 01e0c48013067d10
@impl-hash: 56478213dcceeeb9
@generated: 2026-07-01T20:36:49Z

## `src/osoji` — Core Package

The `osoji` package is a shadow documentation engine and multi-phase code quality audit system. It generates LLM-driven "shadow docs" (concise machine-readable summaries of source files), then runs a 5.5-phase audit pipeline that cross-references those docs against live code to surface dead code, stale documentation, string-contract violations, dead dependencies, dead CI/CD elements, orphaned files, and doc accuracy errors.

---

## Architectural Layers

### 1 — Configuration & Discovery
**`config.py`** is the root of all path resolution. `Config` (the central dataclass) encodes a 5-level provider/model precedence chain (CLI → env → project TOML → global TOML → builtin defaults). Every other module receives a `Config` and derives its paths from it (shadow root, findings dir, symbols dir, analysis root, etc.).

**`walker.py`** discovers source files (`discover_files`) and directories (`discover_directories`) with git-aware or rglob traversal, applying ignore patterns and `.osojiignore`. Results are sorted deepest-first (children before parents) to support bottom-up directory processing.

**`hasher.py`** provides SHA-256 hashing, binary-safe file reading, and the staleness primitives (`@source-hash`, `@impl-hash`, `@children-hash`) embedded in shadow doc headers. `is_findings_current()` gates whether cached findings JSON is still valid.

---

### 2 — Shadow Doc Generation (`shadow.py`)
The generative core. Orchestrates the full shadow doc pipeline:
- File staleness detection (source hash + implementation hash)
- LLM calls via `generate_file_shadow_doc_async` (forced `submit_shadow_doc` tool call)
- Large-file chunking (> 150K tokens → per-chunk generation + `_generate_chunk_rollup_async`)
- Sidecar JSON writes: `.findings.json`, `.symbols.json`, `.signature.json`, `.facts.json`
- AST plugin fact merging (via `plugins/`) — when ava
